description: Add a scope-relative JsonPathExpression helper for plan compilation. Acceptance: given DbTableModel.JsonScope (absolute) and a DbColumnModel.SourceJsonPath (absolute), produce a scope-relative JsonPathExpression by stripping the scope's prefix segments. If the column path exactly equals the scope path, return canonical value-at-scope "$" (zero segments). Validate the scope is a true prefix (or equal) and that no [*] segments remain after stripping; fail fast otherwise.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for: exact scope match -> "$"; scope-not-prefix failure; leftover [*] failure

description: Compile deterministic stored-column bindings for a single table (no SQL yet). Acceptance: for a DbTableModel, enumerate stored/writable columns (ColumnStorage.Stored only) in deterministic order, derive WriteColumnBinding.ParameterName using PlanNamingConventions, and derive WriteValueSource using: DocumentId, ParentKeyPart(i), Ordinal, Scalar(scope-relative path + scalar type), DocumentReference(binding index), DescriptorReference(descriptor resource + scope-relative path), and Precomputed (only as a placeholder for storage-only columns until key-unification compilation is added). UnifiedAlias columns must be excluded from bindings (fail fast if encountered as writable).
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that assert: binding order is deterministic; parameter names are stable; scalar paths are compiled as scope-relative (including "$" value-at-scope)

description: Emit InsertSql for a table from its compiled bindings. Acceptance: emitted InsertSql uses SimpleInsertSqlEmitter with ordered columns matching TableWritePlan.ColumnBindings order and ordered parameters matching WriteColumnBinding.ParameterName order. SQL must be canonicalized (\n only, stable indentation, dialect quoting) and byte-for-byte stable across runs.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that compile the same model twice (and with permuted input ordering) and assert identical InsertSql for both pgsql and mssql

description: Emit UpdateSql for 1:1 tables. Acceptance: emit UpdateSql for any table whose key contains no ColumnKind.Ordinal, but only when at least one stored non-key column exists to set. UpdateSql must use key columns in key order and must reuse the same parameter names already assigned in ColumnBindings (no re-derivation). SQL must be canonicalized and stable for both dialects.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering: (a) UpdateSql present for root and root-scope _ext 1:1 tables with non-key columns, (b) UpdateSql omitted when only key columns exist

description: Emit DeleteByParentSql for replace semantics. Acceptance: for every non-root table plan (any table other than resourceModel.Root), emit DeleteByParentSql with WHERE predicates on exactly the table key columns excluding the table's own ColumnKind.Ordinal (if present), in key order. Parameter names in DeleteByParentSql must reuse the same bare parameter names as the corresponding key columns' WriteColumnBinding.ParameterName. Root-scope _ext 1:1 tables must also emit DeleteByParentSql that deletes by DocumentId (so runtime can remove the row when $._ext.{p} is absent). SQL must be canonicalized and stable for both dialects.
steps-to-verify: Add/adjust unit tests covering delete predicates for: (a) a direct child table, (b) a nested child table, and (c) a root-scope _ext 1:1 table

description: Compute BulkInsertBatching for every compiled TableWritePlan. Acceptance: BulkInsertBatchingInfo is derived via PlanWriteBatchingConventions from ColumnBindings.Count (parameters per row) and stored on each TableWritePlan. Batch size derivation must fail fast when a table's parameter width exceeds the dialect limit. Add coverage for both pgsql and mssql.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests validating batching calculations for representative small and large column counts

description: Compile ResourceWritePlan.TablePlansInDependencyOrder for multi-table resources. Acceptance: for each ResourceStorageKind.RelationalTables resource, compile a TableWritePlan for every DbTableModel in TablesInDependencyOrder, preserving that order. Each TableWritePlan must include InsertSql, optional UpdateSql, optional DeleteByParentSql, BulkInsertBatching, ColumnBindings, and (temporarily) empty KeyUnificationPlans. Compilation must be deterministic under input-order permutations; failures must throw with actionable diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Update/extend compiler unit tests to include a multi-table resource and assert: one table plan per table in dependency order

description: Compile KeyUnificationWritePlan inventory for tables with key unification classes. Acceptance: for each DbTableModel.KeyUnificationClasses entry, emit exactly one KeyUnificationWritePlan with CanonicalColumn and CanonicalBindingIndex pointing to a WriteValueSource.Precomputed binding for that canonical stored column. Emit MembersInOrder matching KeyUnificationClass.MemberPathColumns order; each member must record MemberPathColumn, scope-relative RelativePath ("$" allowed), and (when present) PresenceColumn and PresenceBindingIndex.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering both ScalarMember and DescriptorMember compilation and ordering invariants

description: Detect and validate synthetic presence columns. Acceptance: PresenceIsSynthetic is true iff PresenceColumn's DbColumnModel.SourceJsonPath is null. When PresenceIsSynthetic is true, validate hard that: (1) the presence column is nullable boolean/bit (RelationalScalarType kind bool + IsNullable true), and (2) the table has a matching TableConstraint.NullOrTrue for that column; otherwise throw. If PresenceIsSynthetic is false, treat it as reference-site presence (e.g., ..._DocumentId) and do not require NullOrTrue.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for: synthetic presence happy path; missing NullOrTrue constraint failure; non-synthetic presence path

description: Enforce key-unification write-plan invariants (fail fast). Acceptance: (1) UnifiedAlias columns are never bound or written: exclude from ColumnBindings and from emitted SQL column lists; any attempt to include them fails compilation. (2) Canonical stored columns and synthetic presence-flag columns must be WriteValueSource.Precomputed. (3) Every WriteValueSource.Precomputed binding must be produced by exactly one KeyUnificationWritePlan; orphan precomputed bindings fail compilation. (4) If a table has any Precomputed bindings, it must have key-unification inventory that accounts for them (treat Precomputed as key-unification-only).
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for: UnifiedAlias write exclusion failure; orphan Precomputed failure; duplicate producer failure; canonical column must be Precomputed

description: Compile write plans for every ResourceStorageKind.RelationalTables resource in MappingSetCompiler. Acceptance: MappingSetCompiler uses the new multi-table write-plan compiler for all relational-table resources (including resources with child tables, _ext tables, and key unification) and populates WritePlansByResource for all of them. Any invariant violation during write-plan compilation throws and fails the whole mapping set. RootOnlyReadPlanCompiler behavior remains unchanged: read plans are compiled only for thin-slice supported resources and omitted otherwise.
steps-to-verify: Update MappingSetCompilerTests to assert: write plan exists for a non-root-only resource; read plan is null/omitted for that resource until story 05

description: Update GetWritePlanOrThrow messaging now that write plans exist for all relational-table resources. Acceptance: after this story, missing write plans for ResourceStorageKind.RelationalTables should be treated as an internal compilation/selection bug (throw InvalidOperationException with mapping set key + resource). SharedDescriptorTable resources should continue to throw NotSupportedException pointing to the descriptor write story. Read-plan lookup behavior and next-story pointers remain unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Update/extend MappingSetLookupExtensionsTests to cover: descriptor write-plan message; relational-table missing-plan treated as bug

description: Generalize mappingset.manifest.json resource inclusion and null plan representation. Acceptance: the manifest emitter includes every resource deterministically and emits both write_plan and read_plan properties as either an object or explicit null (never omit the property). Ordering must be deterministic: mapping sets by key; resources by (project_name, resource_name).
steps-to-verify: Update manifest emitter tests to assert property presence and determinism under input permutation

description: Emit write-plan table inventory in mappingset.manifest.json. Acceptance: for write_plan, emit table_plans_in_dependency_order, each with insert_sql_sha256, update_sql_sha256, delete_by_parent_sql_sha256, bulk_insert_batching, column_bindings_in_order (including write_value_source details), and key_unification_plans. Tables must be in dependency order; bindings must be in binding order; key-unification members must preserve member order (do not sort). SQL hashes must be computed over normalized multiline SQL.
steps-to-verify: Update/extend manifest emitter tests to validate table plan arrays, hashes, and ordering invariants

description: Update the existing minimal thin-slice golden manifest to the generalized schema. Acceptance: Fixtures/runtime-plan-compilation/minimal/expected/mappingset.manifest.json matches the new schema and remains stable across runs for both pgsql and mssql.
steps-to-verify: Run (to refresh goldens when needed): UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj

description: Implement a multi-project plans fixture builder for plan compilation tests. Acceptance: fixture folder contains inputs/ with multiple ApiSchema files plus a tiny fixture manifest listing file names and isExtensionProject flags. Builder loads all inputs, builds an EffectiveSchemaSet with correct IsExtensionProject flags, and compiles DerivedRelationalModelSet for both dialects. Output must be deterministic even when the fixture manifest input order is permuted.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for the multi-project fixture builder (including determinism under input order reversal)

description: Add a collections + nested collections + _ext fixture input set. Acceptance: add a base project resource schema for a small readable resource with addresses[*] and addresses[*].periods[*] plus a few scalars. Add an extension project (IsExtensionProject=true, isResourceExtension=true) that adds _ext.sample fields at: (a) root, (b) inside addresses[*], and (c) inside periods[*]. Include a fixture manifest referencing both inputs.
steps-to-verify: Build the DerivedRelationalModelSet from the fixture for pgsql and mssql and assert extension tables are derived and key-aligned to base scopes

description: Add a golden runtime plan compilation test for the collections + _ext fixture. Acceptance: expected mappingset.manifest.json (pgsql + mssql) includes write plans with correct DeleteByParentSql hashes for child and extension tables, deterministic binding order, batching metadata, and table plans in dependency order. Read_plan must be explicit null for this resource until story 05.
steps-to-verify: Run (to refresh goldens when needed): UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj

description: Add a key-unification presence-gating fixture input set. Acceptance: add a small base project resource schema with two optional descriptor endpoints constrained by an equality constraint such that derivation produces: (a) a canonical stored column with SourceJsonPath null, and (b) synthetic ..._Present columns with SourceJsonPath null and TableConstraint.NullOrTrue constraints. Include a fixture manifest and keep the schema minimal and readable.
steps-to-verify: Build the DerivedRelationalModelSet from the fixture and assert the derived table includes canonical + synthetic presence columns and NullOrTrue constraints

description: Add golden tests for the key-unification presence-gating fixture, plus a negative test. Acceptance: expected mappingset.manifest.json shows Precomputed bindings for canonical + synthetic presence columns, compiled KeyUnificationPlans with PresenceIsSynthetic=true, and stable SQL hashes. Add a deterministic negative test that fails compilation when a synthetic presence column is missing its NullOrTrue constraint (model/derivation bug).
steps-to-verify: Run (to refresh goldens when needed): UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj

description: Resolve UpdateSql scope drift: UpdateSql is supported for any 1:1 table (no Ordinal key column), not just the root table. Acceptance: update design docs and story docs to match this rule and remove all remaining "root-only" UpdateSql language. Minimum updates: reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/02-plan-contracts-and-deterministic-bindings.md, reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/04-write-plan-compiler-collections-and-extensions.md, reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/flattening-reconstitution.md, reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/compiled-mapping-set.md, and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs XML docs. Ensure docs clarify how non-root 1:1 tables (including root-scope _ext) are executed: InsertSql for new rows, UpdateSql when the scoped row exists, and DeleteByParentSql when the scoped object is absent.
steps-to-verify: Run: rg -n "UpdateSql\b" reference/design/backend-redesign | rg -n "root" (ensure no remaining "root-only" UpdateSql references)

description: Rename RootOnlyWritePlanCompiler and tests to reflect full write-plan compilation (not root-only). Acceptance: class/file names, public APIs, and comments accurately describe behavior: Compile compiles all relational-table resources across TablesInDependencyOrder; UpdateSql is emitted for any 1:1 table; DeleteByParentSql is emitted for all non-root tables. Update all references (including MappingSetCompiler and unit/golden tests) and ensure no misleading "root-only" or stale "thin-slice gated" comments remain (including the header comment and MappingSetCompiler summary). Update filenames to match.
steps-to-verify: Run: rg -n "RootOnlyWritePlanCompiler" src/dms/backend (should be 0 after rename except historical docs if any)

description: Update write thin-slice gating to match current compiler capability and intended runtime behavior. Acceptance: ThinSliceWritePlanSupportEvaluator (and any IsSupported/TryCompile wrappers) no longer claims key-unification or multi-table resources are unsupported if full compilation supports them. Either (a) repurpose the evaluator to express current execution/feature gating with correct reasons and messaging, or (b) remove the evaluator and the IsSupported/TryCompile surface entirely if unused, updating MappingSetCompiler/tests accordingly. Ensure any remaining gating is used by production code (not test-only) and is covered by unit tests.

Fix misleading root-only wording in code docstrings and comments after the UpdateSql scope decision. Acceptance: src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/SimpleUpdateSqlEmitter.cs summary and any other in-repo docstrings/comments describe UpdateSql as "1:1 tables (no Ordinal)" rather than "root-table". Ensure comment text matches current MappingSetCompiler behavior and plan contracts.
steps-to-verify: Run: rg -n "root-table|root only|root-only" src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans (review and update any misleading matches)

description: Update write-plan lookup exception messaging to remove confusing "thin-slice" phrasing for write plans. Acceptance: MappingSetLookupExtensions write-plan failures describe the current compilation model (compiled relational-table write plans for RelationalTables resources; descriptors have a different write path) and provide actionable next-story pointers without referring to write plans as thin-slice.
steps-to-verify: Run: rg -n "thin-slice" src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/MappingSetLookupExtensions.cs (write-plan messaging should not use "thin-slice")

description: Remove duplication in TryEmitUpdateSql and TryEmitDeleteByParentSql. Acceptance: extract a helper that builds a parameter-name lookup from bindings (e.g., Dictionary<DbColumnName,string>) and reuse it in both emitters. Remove redundant ColumnStorage.Stored checks where bindings are already stored-only, without changing emitted SQL or exceptions. Update/keep unit tests to lock identical SQL output and error messages.
steps-to-verify: Run: rg -n "new Dictionary<DbColumnName, string>\(" src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs (ensure duplication is removed)

description: Optimize ValidateWritableKeyColumns to avoid repeated linear scans. Acceptance: build a Dictionary<DbColumnName, DbColumnModel> once per table (or reuse an existing map) and use it to validate key columns. Preserve identical failure behavior and messages (key column missing, key column is UnifiedAlias).

Harden key-unification compilation against model regressions by asserting member-path columns are UnifiedAlias. Acceptance: during CompileKeyUnificationPlans, validate each KeyUnificationClass.MemberPathColumns column has ColumnStorage.UnifiedAlias (fail fast with a deterministic message if not). Add a unit test that constructs a model with a non-UnifiedAlias member path column and asserts deterministic InvalidOperationException.

Fail fast when a KeyUnificationClass member-path column kind is unsupported. Acceptance: WritePlanCompiler key-unification compilation must reject any member-path column whose DbColumnModel.Kind is not ColumnKind.Scalar or ColumnKind.DescriptorFk. The thrown InvalidOperationException must include the table name, member column name, and actual kind. Add a unit test that constructs a model with a KeyUnificationClass whose MemberPathColumns include a ColumnKind.DocumentFk (or ParentKeyPart) and assert deterministic failure.
steps-to-verify: Add/adjust unit tests in WritePlanCompilerTests to cover unsupported member kinds for key unification

description: Validate table key-column kind invariants and tighten DeleteByParentSql scoping to the parent-key prefix. Acceptance: WritePlanCompiler must (a) validate that DbTableModel.Key.Columns contain only ColumnKind.ParentKeyPart and ColumnKind.Ordinal, and (b) emit DeleteByParentSql predicates for non-root tables using exactly the ParentKeyPart key columns in key order (DocumentId + any parent ordinals), never including the table's own Ordinal or any other key kinds. Add a unit test that introduces an illegal key column kind (e.g., Scalar) and asserts deterministic failure. Ensure existing delete-by-parent SQL output remains byte-for-byte identical for current fixtures (child, nested child, and root-scope _ext tables).
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for: (a) invalid key kind causes deterministic InvalidOperationException, (b) existing DeleteByParentSql output remains unchanged for representative models

description: Enforce StorageKind consistency between ConcreteResourceModel and RelationalResourceModel during mapping set compilation. Acceptance: MappingSetCompiler must validate concreteResourceModel.StorageKind equals concreteResourceModel.RelationalModel.StorageKind; on mismatch, throw InvalidOperationException with a deterministic message naming the resource and both values. MappingSetCompiler must use ConcreteResourceModel.StorageKind for write-plan dispatch (compile write plans for RelationalTables only; omit SharedDescriptorTable). Add a unit test that constructs a mismatched model and asserts deterministic failure.
steps-to-verify: Add a MappingSetCompilerTests case that creates a ConcreteResourceModel with mismatched StorageKind values and asserts the error message

description: Precompute descriptor-edge and document-reference binding lookups during write-plan compilation. Acceptance: WritePlanCompiler must avoid per-column O(N) scans over RelationalResourceModel.DocumentReferenceBindings and DescriptorEdgeSources by building deterministic lookup dictionaries keyed by (DbTableName, DbColumnName). Preserve identical behavior for duplicates and missing entries: still throw InvalidOperationException with the same semantics (and preferably the same messages). No change to emitted SQL or binding inventories in existing unit and golden tests.
steps-to-verify: Run: set UPDATE_GOLDENS=0 and ensure golden fixture tests still pass without regenerating expected manifests

description: Fail fast on duplicate column names when building internal maps in WritePlanCompiler. Acceptance: when constructing columnByName / bindingIndexByColumn / parameterNameByColumn maps, use TryAdd (or equivalent) and throw a deterministic InvalidOperationException if a duplicate DbColumnName is encountered. The exception must include the table name and the duplicate column name. Add a unit test that constructs a DbTableModel with duplicate column names and asserts deterministic failure.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests in WritePlanCompilerTests for duplicate column-name failure behavior

description: Eliminate linear scans for resource-model lookup during plan lookup. Acceptance: MappingSetLookupExtensions.GetConcreteResourceModelOrThrow must become O(1) by using a precomputed dictionary keyed by QualifiedResourceName (either stored on MappingSet or built once per mapping set). Preserve identical exception behavior when a resource is missing from the mapping set. Add a unit test that exercises the dictionary path and asserts results are identical to the current scan-based behavior.
steps-to-verify: Update MappingSetLookupExtensions (and MappingSet if needed) to store or compute a resource->ConcreteResourceModel index

description: Deduplicate runtime-plan compilation golden fixture boilerplate. Acceptance: introduce a shared helper that encapsulates expected/actual path resolution, optional UPDATE_GOLDENS regeneration, writing the actual manifest, and producing the diff output. Migrate CollectionsNestedExtensionRuntimePlanCompilationGoldenFixtureTests and KeyUnificationPresenceGatingRuntimePlanCompilationGoldenFixtureTests to use the helper with no behavior change and no diffs in emitted manifests.
steps-to-verify: Optionally run with UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 and confirm expected manifests regenerate identically

description: Introduce an explicit concept of writability for stored columns to decouple ColumnStorage.Stored from write eligibility. Acceptance: extend the derived relational model to distinguish stored-but-non-writable columns from writable stored columns (e.g., DbColumnModel.IsWritable or a write-behavior enum). Update WritePlanCompiler to compile ColumnBindings only for writable stored columns (plus required key columns and key-unification precomputed targets). Add unit tests proving that a stored non-writable column is excluded from ColumnBindings and does not appear in emitted InsertSql/UpdateSql column lists. Ensure existing fixtures continue to produce identical manifests.
steps-to-verify: Update relational model builder passes to populate the new writability metadata deterministically

description: Harden root-table detection for DeleteByParentSql emission. Acceptance: WritePlanCompiler must determine root-table identity for delete-by-parent emission using stable semantics (e.g., JsonScope == "$" and/or matching Root.Table), not DbTableModel positional-record equality. Root table plans must have DeleteByParentSql == null. All non-root tables (including root-scope _ext 1:1 tables) must have non-null DeleteByParentSql. Add a deterministic validation that the resource model contains exactly one root-scope table and that it corresponds to resourceModel.Root; fail fast with an actionable error if invariants are violated.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that (a) prove root DeleteByParentSql remains null even when resourceModel.Root and TablesInDependencyOrder[0] are not record-equal but represent the same root scope, and (b) assert deterministic failure when no "$"-scope table exists (or more than one exists)

description: Do not treat DocumentFk columns as Precomputed when SourceJsonPath is null. Acceptance: for ColumnKind.DocumentFk stored columns, WritePlanCompiler must always bind WriteValueSource.DocumentReference using the resource's DocumentReferenceBindings lookup and must fail fast with deterministic messages for missing/duplicate bindings, regardless of SourceJsonPath. Add unit tests for a DocumentFk with SourceJsonPath == null and for missing/duplicated bindings to ensure behavior and messages remain correct.
steps-to-verify: Add/adjust WritePlanCompilerTests to cover DocumentFk binding behavior when SourceJsonPath is null and to assert deterministic missing/duplicate-binding errors

description: Make Precomputed binding assumptions explicit and actionable. Acceptance: WritePlanCompiler must emit WriteValueSource.Precomputed only for explicitly supported precompute targets (canonical key-unification columns and synthetic presence columns, and any future explicitly declared roles). If a stored, writable, non-key column has SourceJsonPath == null but is not an allowed precompute target, compilation must fail fast with a deterministic error message describing how to fix (e.g., mark IsWritable=false or add a producer plan). Add unit tests that assert this failure path and message.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that construct a table with an unexpected stored/writable SourceJsonPath==null non-key column and assert deterministic failure with an actionable message

description: Fail fast on duplicate resources when building the ConcreteResourcesInNameOrder lookup index in MappingSetLookupExtensions. Acceptance: MappingSetLookupExtensions.GetConcreteResourceModelOrThrow must throw InvalidOperationException when ConcreteResourcesInNameOrder contains duplicate QualifiedResourceName entries (instead of silently keeping the first). The message must include the mapping set key and the duplicated resource name. Add a unit test that constructs a MappingSet with duplicate concrete-resource entries and asserts deterministic failure.
steps-to-verify: Add a MappingSetLookupExtensionsTests case that builds a MappingSet with duplicated ConcreteResourcesInNameOrder resources and asserts deterministic InvalidOperationException message

description: Refactor WritePlanCompiler into smaller components and reuse a per-table compilation context. Acceptance: extract repeated per-table maps (columnByName, bindingIndexByColumn, parameterNameByColumn, keyColumnNames, requiredPrecomputedColumns) into a single immutable context created once per table and passed through helper methods; extract key-unification plan compilation into its own class. No behavior changes: emitted SQL hashes, ColumnBindings ordering, and exception messages remain identical; all unit and golden fixture tests pass.
steps-to-verify: Run: set UPDATE_GOLDENS=0 and ensure golden fixture tests pass with no manifest diffs

description: Simplify WriteSourceLookup duplicate handling in WritePlanCompiler. Acceptance: replace the dual "dictionary + duplicate-key-set" pattern with a single lookup API that preserves current deterministic error semantics for missing/duplicate document-reference bindings and descriptor edge sources (e.g., "Multiple document-reference bindings match ...", "No descriptor edge source matches ..."). Reduce duplicated code paths without changing emitted SQL/bindings. Existing tests asserting duplicate/missing behavior must continue to pass with the same messages.
steps-to-verify: Ensure WritePlanCompilerTests cases for missing and duplicated document-reference bindings and descriptor edge sources still pass (including message matching)

description: Fail fast on duplicate write-source inventory keys during write-plan compilation. Acceptance: WritePlanCompiler must validate that RelationalResourceModel.DocumentReferenceBindings contains no duplicate (Table, FkColumn) keys and that RelationalResourceModel.DescriptorEdgeSources contains no duplicate (Table, FkColumn) keys. If duplicates are found, compilation must throw InvalidOperationException before compiling any TableWritePlan, with a deterministic message that names the resource and the duplicated key(s) (and counts, if available). This validation must trigger even when no compiled ColumnBindings reference the duplicated entries (i.e., do not rely on lazy lookup-time detection).
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that construct a RelationalResourceModel with duplicate DocumentReferenceBindings/DescriptorEdgeSources keys that are not referenced by any table columns and assert deterministic InvalidOperationException messages

description: Decouple synthetic presence detection from SourceJsonPath null alone. Acceptance: KeyUnificationWritePlanCompiler must derive PresenceIsSynthetic using stable semantic signals (e.g., ColumnKind.Scalar + nullable boolean + SourceJsonPath == null), and must never classify reference-site presence columns (the reference group's DocumentFk column) as synthetic even if SourceJsonPath is null. Preserve existing behavior for true synthetic presence flags: require nullable boolean type, and require a matching TableConstraint.NullOrTrue. Add a regression unit test that mutates a reference-site presence column to SourceJsonPath == null and asserts it is not treated as synthetic (no nullable-boolean validation or NullOrTrue requirement is applied).
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that (a) confirm synthetic presence flags are still recognized and validated, and (b) confirm reference-site presence columns are never treated as synthetic even when SourceJsonPath is null

description: Reduce duplicated internal map/lookup construction helpers in WritePlanCompiler. Acceptance: introduce a small shared helper for building per-table maps (columnByName, bindingIndexByColumn, parameterNameByColumn) and write-source lookup maps with deterministic duplicate handling. Prefer immutable/frozen dictionaries for maps that are read-only after construction. No behavior changes: emitted SQL must remain byte-for-byte identical, ColumnBindings order must remain unchanged, and all existing exception messages (except any explicitly improved duplicate diagnostics) must remain stable. Update/keep unit and golden fixture tests to lock down output stability.
steps-to-verify: Run: set UPDATE_GOLDENS=0 and ensure golden fixture tests pass with no manifest diffs

description: Split WritePlanCompilerTests into smaller focused fixtures. Acceptance: refactor WritePlanCompilerTests.cs into multiple test files/classes grouped by feature area (bindings/parameter naming, insert SQL, update SQL, delete-by-parent SQL, batching, key-unification). Preserve all existing coverage and assertions (including message matching and deterministic output comparisons). Test names and file names must follow existing NUnit conventions (Given_* fixtures and It_* tests).

Rename thin-slice test helpers that now emit/validate full mapping-set manifests. Acceptance: rename ThinSliceMappingSetManifestJsonEmitter and ThinSliceFixtureModelSetBuilder (and related test naming) to reflect their current role (mapping-set manifest emission and fixture model-set building beyond thin-slice). Update filenames, classes, and all references. Ensure no remaining misleading "ThinSlice" references for these helpers (excluding intentionally thin-slice read-plan support types).
steps-to-verify: Run: rg -n "ThinSliceMappingSetManifestJsonEmitter|ThinSliceFixtureModelSetBuilder" src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit (ensure no references remain after rename)

description: Add an authoritative ds-5.2 runtime plan compilation golden manifest test for this story’s write-plan coverage. Acceptance: compile mapping sets (pgsql + mssql) from src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative/ds-5.2/inputs/ds-5.2-api-schema-authoritative.json (no duplicated copy under the test project), emit mappingset.manifest.json via the mapping-set manifest emitter, and compare expected vs actual under src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Fixtures/authoritative/ds-5.2/expected/mappingset.manifest.json. The manifest must reflect full write-plan compilation across child tables (DeleteByParentSql hashes for non-root tables) with deterministic ordering and normalized SQL hashes; descriptor resources must continue to have write_plan=null.
steps-to-verify: Run (to refresh goldens when needed): UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj

description: Add an authoritative ds-5.2 + sample extension runtime plan compilation golden manifest test for this story’s _ext table coverage. Acceptance: compile mapping sets (pgsql + mssql) from both authoritative ApiSchema inputs: src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative/ds-5.2/inputs/ds-5.2-api-schema-authoritative.json (core) plus src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative/sample/inputs/sample-api-schema-authoritative.json (IsExtensionProject=true). Emit and golden-compare mappingset.manifest.json under src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Fixtures/authoritative/sample/expected/mappingset.manifest.json. The manifest must include write plans for extension tables (root + child scopes) with correct DeleteByParentSql hashes for non-root tables and deterministic normalized SQL hashes/binding metadata.
steps-to-verify: Run (to refresh goldens when needed): UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj
